name: Mratinje Dam (Piva dam)
description: The 220m-high arch dam that created turquoise Piva Lake in 1975, between Plužine and the Šćepan Polje border. The single most-cited pull-over on the Piva canyon drive — you can view it from the top and from below.
communityNotes: Repeatedly named as the highlight stop of the canyon road. TripAdvisor: 'worth visiting from top and below', and 'the tunnels, all 36 from Pluzine to dam wall and then another 16 to Bosnia border are also amazing.' The road around the lake is carved into the cliff with dozens of short tunnels; the dam is the natural place to stop, stretch and photograph the emerald reservoir. ⚡ This is a roadside viewpoint/pull-over (~20 min), not a standalone destination — the canyon drive itself is the experience.
cost: free
bestTime: Day 8 (Jun 23) — roadside pullover on the Plužine stretch. The 56 tunnels are half the experience; the dam wall is the natural photo stop with the emerald reservoir below.

name: Etno Selo Grab (Šćepan Polje rafting camp)
description: Ethno-village + rafting camp on a private Tara River beach right at the Šćepan Polje border — the natural overnight + rafting base for the day you cross from Bosnia. Wooden chalets and pitches, restaurant/bar hanging over the river. ~9km in from the border road (6km asphalt + 2km gravel).
communityNotes: Rated ~8.7/10 (4.6★). 'The camp is set in a stunning location' (Caroline C., Jul 2024); 'The absolute isolation and tranquility of the valley' (Matjaž J., Jul 2023); reviewers rave about 'colossal food portions' and 'down to earth, friendly' staff. Runs the classic Brštanovica → Šćepan Polje rafting trip (~13-18km). Note: access to the riverside is down a long flight of stairs — fine for this group, not for limited mobility.
cost: rooms/chalets ~€45-151/night; Tara rafting ~€70 half-day, ~€150 full-day w/ lunch
facilities: hot showers, restaurant/bar, free WiFi, free parking, fire pit, private Tara river beach
bestTime: Night 8 (Jun 23) — natural base after Tara rafting take-out at Šćepan Polje. Book: tara-grab.com. 6km asphalt + 2km gravel from the border road.

name: Bobotov Kuk (2523m) — Montenegro's highest hike
description: Durmitor's iconic 2523m summit, doable as a long day hike for fit hikers with a thrilling final scramble on exposed rock secured by fixed steel cables. The fit-hiker standard is to start high at Sedlo pass; the Black Lake start is a much bigger 24km / ~10h day.
communityNotes: Three options people use: (1) SEDLO PASS out-and-back ~10km, 4-5h, easiest/most popular (drive or ~€15 shared taxi to the trailhead); (2) SEDLO → ŽABLJAK traverse ~17km ~7h, often called the best (needs a car shuttle); (3) BLACK LAKE return ~24km ~10h, hardest. Summit is '500m of scrambling on exposed rock... a little sketchy at times' (The Sandy Feet), secured by fixed cables. LATE-JUNE SNOW IS THE REAL ISSUE: 'In early June about 60% of the trail was still covered in snow', lingering into July on the higher Žabljak-side route. For late June: prefer the Sedlo start, ASK THE NP WARDEN about conditions, and be ready to turn back if snowfields block the upper scramble.
cost: €3-5 pp Durmitor NP fee (collected by warden at Sedlo); ~€15 shared taxi to Sedlo
timeNeeded: 4-5h from Sedlo / ~7h Sedlo→Žabljak / ~10h from Black Lake
bestTime: late June can still hold snow — verify locally; start early

name: Ćurevac viewpoint (Tara Canyon)
description: Cliff-edge viewpoint (~1626m) over the deepest part of the Tara Canyon (Europe's deepest gorge, ~1300m). Easy half-day add-on: ~20min drive from Žabljak to trailhead parking (43.1939, 19.0896), then a 30-45min easy walk to the rim.
communityNotes: Low-effort, high-reward and even 'suitable for children.' Road Tripster: 'Arriving at the viewpoint you have a superb view over the surrounding landscape with the Tara River passing by in the canyon below' — bring binoculars, the river is far below. ~2.1km out-and-back, ~170m gain. Drive 7.6km from Žabljak following signs toward Tepca / 'vidikovac', staying on the bigger road at unmarked splits. The perfect bad-weather / rest-day option when a big peak is off.
cost: €3-5 pp NP entry
bestTime: morning light / clear weather for the canyon

name: Sedlo Pass scenic drive (P14 Durmitor Ring)
description: At ~1907m, the highest paved pass in Montenegro and the centerpiece of the P14 'Durmitor Ring' between Plužine and Žabljak — rolling alpine meadows under jagged peaks and glacial 'mountain-eye' lakes. Also the standard high trailhead for Bobotov Kuk. Our route arrives on this very road, so it's essentially on-route for free. Pass coordinate is approximate (the pass is a stretch, not a point).
communityNotes: Widely praised as the most scenic part of the Durmitor Ring ('the best road trip I've ever done'). REAL ROAD WARNINGS: 'totally paved but full of potholes... very narrow, requires at least one car to pull over to pass... unlit tunnels (extremely dusty)... very steep with some dangerous drop-offs without protections.' Fine in a normal rental in summer, just drive slowly. Open and a highlight in late June (closed roughly mid-Nov onward).
bestTime: summer; clear day for the meadow/peak views

name: Tara Canyon rafting — Brštanovica section
description: Whitewater rafting in Europe's deepest canyon. The exciting, popular run is the ~18km Brštanovica → Šćepan Polje section with 21 of the Tara's ~50 rapids; most operators put in near the Đurđevića Tara bridge. Coordinate = the bridge (meeting points cluster here and at Šćepan Polje).
communityNotes: Most operators run the Brštanovica section, 'technically the most demanding... 21 of 50 cascades and the nicest waterfall.' LATE-JUNE WATER: high, fast, COLD (~10-12°C), 'most adrenaline-y time' — wetsuits compulsory. ⚠️ BOOK URGENTLY for Jun 23: email office@raftingtara.com (Rafting Centar Drina-Tara, ~€60pp) or info@tarasportrafting.com (TaraSport, ~€50pp). State: 4 adults, Jun 23, 10:00–10:30 start, arriving from Sarajevo via Foča. Within the 2-week window = filling up now.
cost: ~€60 pp (Rafting Centar Drina-Tara, Day 8 = Jun 23); 4 × €60 = €240
timeNeeded: half day (most common) or full day
bestTime: Day 8 (Jun 23) — raft Brštanovica → Šćepan Polje (~18km, 3–4h). Book before Jun 16 via email/phone (see Essentials). Late June = high/fast water; wetsuit required (river ~8°C).

name: Đurđevića Tara Bridge zipline
description: Zipline across the Tara Canyon at the historic 1937-40 Đurđevića Tara bridge (365m long, 172m above the river). The flagship 'Extreme Zipline Tara' is ~1km+ across the gorge at up to 120km/h; a shorter twin-line option lets two fly side by side. ~30min NE of Žabljak, right by the rafting put-in.
communityNotes: Prices reported: ~€20-25 for the long extreme line (some list up to €40), ~€10-30 for shorter ones; +€10 for pro photos. TripAdvisor: 'It goes super fast over the trees and the Tara, the view is beautiful. I highly recommend.' Common gripe: 'beautiful but it ended far too quickly to justify the price,' and quality varies by booth — pick the long line. Great quick combo with rafting at the same spot.
cost: ~€20-25 long extreme line (up to €40 listed); ~€10-30 shorter lines; +€10 photos
timeNeeded: ~30-60min incl. wait; the ride itself is short
bestTime: Day 10 (Jun 25) — on the Žabljak → Ostrog drive, Đurđevića Tara Bridge is a natural stop (~40 min from Žabljak). Open mid-Apr – early Oct, 10:00–19:00. Quick standalone (~20 min) or skip and just photograph the bridge.

name: Auto Camp Mlinski Potok (Žabljak)
description: Top-rated tent campsite at 1472m on the Žabljak→Black Lake road, ~0.6km / ~15min walk from Black Lake — i.e. walkable to the Veliki Međed and Bobotov Kuk trailheads. Transparent à-la-carte pricing, communal kitchen, campfire area. Best all-round Žabljak base.
communityNotes: TripAdvisor 4.7/5, ACSI 9.0/10 — consistently one of the top camps near Žabljak. 'Plenty of toilets and hot showers in a new block'; free WiFi, washing machine, bar/breakfast. Owner praised as 'always helpful.' 'Perhaps the most beautiful part of Europe... Durmitor Žabljak and the Tara Canyon are very special' (Jun 2024). Minor gripe about the shower block (dark at night / no toilet paper).
cost: à la carte (adult €5, big tent €4, car €3, electricity €2.50, tax €1.20pp) → ~€31-37/night for 4 + tent + car
facilities: hot showers (new block), flush toilets, communal kitchen, washing machine, free WiFi, bar/breakfast, campfire; near Black Lake
bestTime: Nights 8–9 (Jun 23–24) — base for the Durmitor hike day. Walk-in OK or email minakamp@gmail.com. 0.6km walk to Black Lake trailhead.

name: Camp Apartments Razvršje (Žabljak)
description: Big family-run camp + apartments in forest ~2km from Žabljak center and ~2km from Black Lake, with its own restaurant and shop. Doubles as a bargain apartment option — the pick if the group wants a roof instead of tents for a night.
communityNotes: TripAdvisor 4.2/5. Owner Mišo is the star: 'an amazingly welcoming and helpful host... set us off on a driving tour he mapped out for us.' Tent camping ~€5pp; apartments are a steal — 'a great two-bedroom apartment with kitchenette for 20 Euros a night,' 'best we've stayed in Montenegro and really good value.' On-site restaurant + grocery, free WiFi, marked trails to Black Lake/peaks; books rafting/zipline/canyoning.
cost: tent ~€5pp (~€20 + car for 4); 2-bed apartment ~€20-25/night — strong cheap-villa-style win
facilities: showers/toilets, on-site restaurant + grocery, free WiFi, partial shade, marked hiking routes; books adventure activities
bestTime: good budget apartment fallback if not camping

name: Virpazar kayak rental (Skadar Lake)
description: Virpazar is the main launch hub for self-guided kayaking on Skadar Lake — rental shacks by the bridge/canal, paddle out through lily-pad channels onto the open lake. The best half-day lake activity for an active group. The drive-up Pavlova Strana viewpoint pairs with it; a motorboat tour is the relaxed alternative.
communityNotes: Consistent rates: single kayak ~€5/hr or ~€20/day, double ~€10/hr or ~€30/day; separate ~€5/person National Park fee at the Virpazar booth. Flat, beginner-friendly water — no experience needed ('calm waters... top-quality kayaks and a comprehensive briefing'). Best in the cooler morning before the boats. For a GUIDED boat/kayak tour: Kingfisher Boat & Kayak (skadarlakeboatcruise.com) is the most-reviewed option — +382 67 261 612 / hello@skadarlakeboatcruise.com; 30min check-in at Virpazar bridge info desk. Also bookable on GetYourGuide. Verdict: kayak = best for this group; Kingfisher boat tour if you'd rather sightsee Grmožur/Kom island monastery.
cost: single ~€5/hr or €20/day, double ~€10/hr or €30/day + ~€5/person NP fee
bestTime: Day 10 (Jun 25) morning — start by 09:00. Flat water, no experience needed. Pairs with Pavlova Strana viewpoint. Kingfisher boat tour (+382 67 261 612) is the guided alternative.

name: Pavlova Strana viewpoint (Skadar Lake)
description: The iconic horseshoe bend where the Rijeka Crnojevića meets Skadar Lake — the most photographed view in the national park. Drive-up viewpoint with a pullout and small restaurant; the half-day Skadar option that needs the least time, just above Rijeka Crnojevića village.
communityNotes: Widely called the best short-visit Skadar experience: 'absolutely breathtaking and easily one of the most stunning viewpoints we saw in Montenegro', 'a FIVE STAR panorama... just like in all the brochures', and several say the view 'is superior to taking a boat trip.' Caveat: the most direct approach road is genuinely scary — 'the switchbacks here were truly scary... curves that required three-point turns'; the longer route via the yellow sign past Meterizi is wider. Best light early morning or late afternoon for reflections. Free, 24/7.
cost: free
bestTime: Day 10 (Jun 25) late afternoon — sunset ~20:22. Drive the wider approach road (not the forest track). Sequence: kayak morning → Virpazar fish lunch → Pavlova Strana golden hour.

name: Vinarija Kopitović (winery near Skadar Lake)
description: Small family winery in the Crmnica hills ~10min from Skadar Lake / 15-20min from the coast, making indigenous Vranac and Krstač. Warm, no-frills cellar visit with a big homemade food platter — the most recommended family-winery experience near the lake.
communityNotes: Reviewed as 'best wine tasting around Lake Skadar': 'the family is really lovely and warm', 'a real family vibe... tradition spanning 300 years', tastings come with 'a plate full of wonderful homemade food — all kinds of cheese, sausages, bruschetta, olives.' Visitors 'stayed more than 3 hours.' No fixed price (arrange directly). Other names travelers mention: Cermeniza (in Virpazar) and Mašanović. Coordinates verified to Gornji Brčeli.
bestTime: easy add-on between Skadar and the coast

name: Njegoš Mausoleum (Lovćen NP)
description: Monumental hilltop mausoleum of poet-ruler Petar II Petrović-Njegoš on Jezerski vrh (~1660m), the second peak of Mt Lovćen. 461 steps up a marble tunnel lead to one of Montenegro's great 360° panoramas — sea, Skadar, Durmitor, and on a clear day Albania/Italy. Pairs with the Kotor serpentine drive.
communityNotes: Consistently rated for the view: '461 steps... worth the view if you can climb and have the time', 'showing nearly all of Montenegro.' Tips travelers repeat: the marble tunnel is 'very cold inside, which helps you not sweat', and 'come down via the hiking trail above the tunnel rather than retracing the steps' for better views. Summit parking is free but tiny and fills fast.
cost: €11 pp total (€3 NP entry + €8 mausoleum, 2025/26 reports) — €44 for 4, still worth it
bestTime: Day 12 (Jun 27) — drive up from Kotor before 09:00 (summer downhill restriction 08:00–15:00). Clear morning = best panorama over Kotor Bay, Skadar and Albania.

name: Kotor Serpentine viewpoint (Kotor–Lovćen road)
description: The famous pullout high on the old Austro-Hungarian Kotor–Njeguši serpentine (25 numbered hairpins), with the postcard top-down view of Kotor's fjord-like bay and the snaking switchbacks below. A drive-and-stop highlight on the way up to Lovćen/Njeguši.
communityNotes: Strong consensus it's worth it but the road takes nerves: 'incredible views of the old town, Kotor bay and beyond', 'just do it, the views are worth it.' Best panorama from the upper switchbacks (after ~no.20). The catch is traffic: 'the street is very narrow and sometimes vans and even coaches are coming your way — it is a game of millimetres.' IMPORTANT: in summer (~May 25–Oct 15) the Njeguši→Kotor DOWNHILL direction is restricted 08:00–15:00, so plan to drive UP from Kotor (or only come down in the evening). Go before 9am or after 4pm; small car advised.
cost: free
bestTime: drive UP from Kotor before 9am or after 4pm (summer downhill 08:00-15:00 restricted)

name: San Giovanni Fortress — official (paid) climb
description: The ~1350-step climb up Kotor's city walls to the Castle of San Giovanni (~260m up) for the iconic Bay of Kotor panorama. Official entrance is inside the old town near the North Gate; tickets are checked at a turnstile.
communityNotes: Price has crept up: multiple 2025 reviewers report €15/person in high season (was €8 a couple of years ago). 'While it is pricey to enter (15 euro per person) the climb was worth it.' Tickets are checked on the way DOWN at a turnstile, so you can't go up the free Ladder trail and exit through the official gate. June 2025 visitor: 'They check tickets on the way down though so you have to return back through the Ladder of Kotor trek.' VERDICT (red-team corrected): €15 high season confirmed, but the 'free before 8am' loophole is unreliable for 2026 — official admission starts ~07:00 and booth staffing varies; gates may be open pre-07:00 but treat free entry as a bonus, not a plan. Budget €60 for 4 or start ~06:00.
cost: €15/person high season confirmed (€60 for 4); pre-07:00 free entry is a bonus, not a plan
bestTime: Day 12 (Jun 27) — start at 07:30 (free before 08:00, 1 confirmed cruise ship arrives ~09:30). Midday is brutally hot and crowded — be at the top by 09:00.

name: Ladder of Kotor — free trail behind the fortress
description: The free serpentine mule track ('Kotorske serpentine' / Ladder of Kotor) that starts OUTSIDE the walls on Tabačina road, north of the Škurda river (~10min from the North Gate). It zig-zags up past the 1518 Church of Our Lady of Remedy to Špiljari village behind the upper fortress, with big bay views.
communityNotes: CRITICAL 2024 UPDATE — the old free loophole is largely closed. The 'window/hole' in the fortress wall that used to let Ladder hikers slip into the paid ramparts is now PERMANENTLY SEALED, and a turnstile was added: 'the hole in the wall (window in reality) is permanently closed.' The TRAIL stays free and open and gives huge bay views from Špiljari, BUT it no longer gets you inside San Giovanni for free. So: do the Ladder as a free scenic hike, but if you want the fortress itself, climb the official route before 8am (free) or pay. Steep, exposed, hot — go early with water.
cost: free (scenic trail only — no longer enters the paid fortress)
timeNeeded: ~45 min up to the Špiljari viewpoint
bestTime: early morning, before heat

name: Perast
description: Tiny baroque waterfront town on the Bay of Kotor, ~15min from Kotor, car-free seafront and the two famous islets just offshore. Photogenic and calm — a good half-day add-on to Kotor.
communityNotes: Parking is the notorious pain point: a paid lot (~€8/day) on the main road above town, or free roadside spots along the ridge if you arrive early — 'it's hard to park nearby, but it's worth a try.' Some attendants find you a space and hook you up with a boat operator. Goes from quiet to busy with the midday Kotor/cruise day-trip crowd — early morning or after ~16:00 is much nicer. ⚡ GROUP FIT NOTE: Mainly photogenic/architectural — 20-30 min of 'nice small town' and then most active groups are done. Skip if time is tight on the Kotor day; it's a photo stop, not a destination.
cost: Our Lady of the Rocks boat €5-10 pp return + €3 church/museum; parking ~€8/day paid lot, or free roadside if you find a spot
timeNeeded: 2-3h incl. boat (island open to ~18:00 in June)
bestTime: Day 12 (Jun 27) — after the Lovćen mausoleum, arrive ~15:00–16:00 when the day-trippers thin. Our Lady of the Rocks boat: buy at the pier, €5-10 return.

name: Our Lady of the Rocks (Gospa od Škrpjela)
description: Man-made islet off Perast, built up over centuries by sinking ships and dropping rocks, topped by a blue-domed church and small museum. Reached by a 5-minute taxi-boat hop from Perast's waterfront.
communityNotes: Boat is cheap and frequent — buy directly from the pier operators, not a pre-booked tour: 'water taxi return trip is 5 EUR and entrance to the island additional 2 EUR' (some quote €10, haggle). Most spend 20-30 min on the island, which is plenty; short 10-15 min waits for the next boat. The neighbouring island of St George is a private monastery — photograph only, no landing.
cost: boat ~€5 return + €2 island/church entry (book at the pier)

name: Budva Old Town bars (Casper, El Mundo, Mañana)
description: The walkable bar cluster inside/around Budva's walled old town — the consistently-good nightlife on the coast, and the warm-up before (or instead of) the big Top Hill club above town. Open-air lounges against the old walls.
communityNotes: Old Town is where the reliably good bars are. Casper (daily 10:00-02:00, live jazz) and El Mundo (open-air lounge against the city walls) are the perennial favorites. Mañana for cocktails. ⚠️ Top Hill (marquee hilltop club) is CLOSED in late June — opens in July. The actual club option: Club Sparta (capacity 600, EDM/R&B, open until sunrise). Reddit: 'overrated/tacky' but good for a night if expectations are set right.
bestTime: Night 11 (Jun 26, Friday) — best bar night of the trip on the Montenegrin coast. Late June = live music at Casper (not DJ nights — those start July); El Mundo open-air. Top Hill opens in July only.
cost: €2-4 per drink

name: Jaz Beach (camping + beach)
description: Long open beach ~3km west of Budva, roomier than the in-town beaches, with camping behind it and a history as a festival site (Sea Dance was here 2014-17, now gone). Mix of concession and free sand.
communityNotes: Less crammed than Mogren/Slovenska. 'The eastern end (toward Budva) is the quietest section, with fewer concessions and more free-sand space.' Free access; loungers ~€8-12 each. ⚠️ CAMPING: Autocamp Jaz consistently rates 1.5/5 — terrible sanitation, noise until 4am, aggressive upselling. Do not camp here. The beach itself is fine for a swim stop.
cost: free entry; loungers ~€8-12 each
bestTime: eastern/Budva end is quietest

name: Lučice Beach (Petrovac)
description: Small pine-backed pebble cove ~10min walk from Petrovac, Blue-Flag water, sheltered bay — one of the prettier swimming beaches on the southern Riviera.
communityNotes: Beautiful and clean BUT small and gets rammed: 'the beach itself & setting is really beautiful, but as many others have said it's incredibly crowded (even at 10am there is essentially no space to put your things).' Public, no entry fee; lounger+umbrella sets ~€15-30 (considered pricey). Verdict: go early or you won't fit. Reachable on foot from Petrovac old town.
cost: free entry; lounger set ~€15-30
bestTime: arrive before ~9-10am for a spot

name: Ploče Beach (party / pools / cliff jumps)
description: A party beach club on a rocky peninsula ~9-10km west of Budva: several saltwater pools cut into the cliffs, DJ/bar, hourly foam parties, and cliff/ladder jumps straight into the sea. A day-drinking, jump-in-the-water vibe rather than a sand beach — the best coast fit for this group.
communityNotes: Known for four outdoor saltwater pools (incl. an adult 'party pool'), 'rock-jump style entries into the water', metal ladders down the cliffs, and 'every hour, foam is released into one of the large pools.' Loud music near the DJ booth; rents catamarans/jet skis, parasailing, diving. Pebbly, so sea shoes help. Sunbed ~€15; reachable by car or a ~€8 boat from Budva. WARNING (red team): recent reviews include a documented triple-overcharging incident at the pool bar plus 'pool is dirty' complaints; €1.20 for 0.5L water. Rule: pay per round in cash, check every bill.
cost: sunbed ~€15; boat from Budva ~€8; pay bar per round in cash, check every bill

name: Žanjic Beach (Luštica) + cliff jumping
description: White-pebble beach in an olive-grove bay on the outer Luštica peninsula, near the mouth of the Bay of Kotor. Clear water great for snorkeling, cliff-jumping spots nearby, and the main pier/hub for Blue Cave and Mamula boat trips.
communityNotes: 'Known for its clear waters and pebble beach... excellent for snorkeling.' Doubles as the boat hub — 'most reputable dive centers offer boat pickups directly from the main pier' for the Blue Cave / Mamula. Cliff jumping reported near Kamp Begović: 'head down to the cliffs to the water, ideal for jumping off.' Quieter and more scenic than the Budva beaches; reached by a winding Luštica road or by boat from Herceg Novi.
timeNeeded: half day (more if combining with Blue Cave)

name: Blue Cave (Plava Špilja), Luštica
description: Sea cave on the SW coast of Luštica with a domed roof and pale seabed that lights the water electric blue. Reachable only by boat/kayak. Reality check: swimming INSIDE is often not permitted or practical — at peak midday 20+ boats jockey inside the cave and operators keep swimmers out; the swim happens just outside. Usually combined with Mamula island, Žanjic and the WWII submarine tunnel.
communityNotes: No entrance fee for the cave, you only pay the boat. Pricing: short direct cave trips ~€15-25/person; half-day combo tours (Blue Cave + Mamula + Žanjic + submarine tunnel) ~€30-60/person, ~3-5h. Boats leave from Herceg Novi, Žanjic and Luštica Bay. TIMING (red-team corrected): the best-light window (10:00-13:00) collides with the boat rush — book the earliest ~09:00 departure from Herceg Novi/Žanjic and accept slightly less glow, or go private. Bring a mask.
cost: boat only: ~€15-25 direct, ~€30-60 combo tour (no cave fee)
bestTime: Day 12 (Jun 27) morning or Day 11 (Jun 26) — taxi boat from Rose village on Luštica peninsula (~40 min from Kotor). Earliest ~09:00 departure; midday = best light but 20+ boats inside the cave.

name: Stari Bar (Old Bar) — abandoned fortress town
description: Sprawling ruined medieval/Ottoman town on a hill ~3km inland from modern Bar, largely abandoned after the 1878 siege and a 1979 earthquake — ~200 stone ruins, a hammam, aqueduct and churches across a huge fortified site with mountain-and-sea views. The single best reason to push into the deep south for a ruins-loving group.
communityNotes: TripAdvisor 4.5/5 over 600+: 'Fairytale place. Entrance was €5pp, worth a visit!', 'romantic ruins', views 'fantastic'. Repeated tips: wear ankle-supporting shoes (uneven/steep), hat + water in summer, go early to dodge cruise crowds. Less hyped than Kotor, so often quiet. Pairs with the ~2,240-year-old Stara Maslina olive tree 1km away (€1, 15-min stop).
cost: ~€4-5pp entry; parking ~€2
bestTime: Day 11 (Jun 26) — first stop en route from Skadar to Budva. Arrive before 10:00 to beat the heat and tourist minibuses from Bar.

name: Ulcinj Old Town (Stari Grad) + Mala Plaža
description: Montenegro's most Albanian, most 'oriental'-feeling town (~60-70% Albanian). A well-preserved medieval citadel on a sea bluff, pirate/legend history, far less hyped than Kotor or Budva. Mala Plaža sits right below, fronted by the pedestrian 'Korzo' of cafés and pizzerias.
communityNotes: Travelers say it's 'rough around the edges' vs Kotor/Budva but 'sufficiently different' to be worth it — praised for sunsets and atmosphere from the fortress walls. Forum experts warn it gets 'very busy in summer and very full on the little beach', and streets climb steeply from the sea — the OLD TOWN, not the tiny beach, is the draw. The distinctly Albanian-Muslim culture is the reason to stop; avoid turning this into a Velika Plaža detour on a moving day.
bestTime: Day 11 (Jun 26) midday — on the way south from Stari Bar to Ada Bojana. Stari Bar → Ulcinj ≈ 35 min, then Ada Bojana +30 min south. Wander the old town ramparts, leave by 13:30 to reach Ada Bojana with full afternoon.

name: Velika Plaža (Long Beach), Ulcinj
description: Montenegro's biggest beach — ~12-13km of flat sand running from Ulcinj to the Bojana river mouth, ~60m wide. Wide-open and far less cramped than Ulcinj's Mala Plaža; the country's top kitesurf/windsurf coast with a string of beach bars and kite centers. Shallow, gently sloping — good for swimming and learning watersports.
communityNotes: Kitesurfers love the space: 'lots of space to launch your kite', 'one of the most beautiful and extensive kite spots in Europe.' Thermal NW side-onshore wind ~15-25 kn most summer afternoons (mornings can be light/offshore), stable May-Sept — but in June thermals can run lighter than mid-summer, so build in buffer time if a kite session is the goal. Caveat people repeat: the 'beach bars aren't budget-friendly.'
bestTime: afternoons for thermal wind; swim mornings

name: Ada Bojana — river island, kitesurf + stilt fish restaurants
description: Triangular river island in the Bojana delta on the Albanian border, with a ~3km sandy beach (officially nudist/clothing-optional, plus regular sections). The flat-water kite spot at the river mouth is the marquee draw, alongside rustic wooden stilt fish restaurants over the water — sunset seafood dinners are the signature experience.
communityNotes: Consistently worth the ~15min hop from Ulcinj. Fish restaurants are the highlight: Restaurant Ada Bojana praised for fish soup, sunset deck and "reasonable prices"; Ćirkova Čarda for genuinely wild-caught fish; Misko the oldest (since 1991). **Nudity note:** Ada Bojana has a nudist heritage (FKK) but clothing is NOT mandatory — multiple reviewers confirm clothed visitors are fully accommodated, non-nudist beach sections exist. Best for kitesurfers + a sunset seafood dinner.
bestTime: sunset for the stilt-restaurant dinner

name: Kiteloop Kiteboarding Center (Ada Bojana)
description: The most-recommended kite school on the coast and Montenegro's only IKO-accredited center, at the Ada Bojana / river-mouth end of Velika Plaža. Lessons, coaching, rental, storage, plus a beach bar/restaurant. Flat, deep water at the river mouth — good for learning. Open ~June-September. Coordinate is representative (kite spots spread along the river-mouth end).
communityNotes: TripAdvisor 4.8/5: 'Amazing place with really kind people and professional staff', 'Learnt so much and it truly is a stunning spot to kitesurf', beach-bar vibe with good veggie/vegan food. Two honest negatives: one couple's 9h course got only ~1.5h of instruction on a low-wind day; an independent rider was 'told to leave' for not booking. Net: great school, but wind is thermal — build in buffer time and don't expect free-ride access without paying.
bestTime: June-Sept; afternoons for thermal wind

name: Camp Full Monte (Sutorina, near Herceg Novi)
description: Tiny (20-pitch) eco / clothing-optional hill campsite in the Sutorina valley, ~25-30 min from Dubrovnik airport just over the Croatian border — a handy first or last night of the Montenegro loop. Off-grid: solar power, composting toilets, solar showers, organic garden, communal home-cooked dinners. Tents only. Coordinate approximate (map sources paywalled) — verify before navigating.
communityNotes: TripAdvisor 4.9/5 over 100+, Travelers' Choice: 'Great owners, super helpful', 'meals & hospitality were top notch', 'most beautiful and peaceful setting', facilities 'spotless.' Run by hosts Steve & Denise; very social communal-dinner atmosphere. Clothing-optional but 'no pressure.' It's 3km uphill off the main road with a 20-tent cap, so BOOK AHEAD.
cost: own tent pitch from ~€12pp; rental tents €15-20pp incl. breakfast
facilities: solar showers, composting toilets, equipped kitchen, spring water, WiFi/4G, organic-garden dinners, hammocks; tents only
bestTime: first or last night of the loop (near Dubrovnik airport); book ahead

name: Auto Camp Maslina (Buljarica, near Petrovac)
description: Shaded coast campsite in an old olive grove (300+ year-old trees) at Buljarica bay, ~2km from Petrovac and ~200m from the long, wild Buljarica beach. One of the better-rated coast camps; clean hot showers, free WiFi, open all year. The budget coast base for a beach night.
communityNotes: Rated ~4.1/5; campers praise the friendliness, value, cleanliness and olive-grove shade, plus the quiet beach nearby — a genuinely budget coast option vs apartments. (Note: the cheaper, more rustic 'Camp Buljarica' beachfront field is right next door — ~€18-22/night for 4 — if you want rock-bottom on the sand.)
cost: high season ~€6 adult + €4 small tent + €4 car (+€3 electric, +€1 tax) → ~€30-35/night for 4 + car + tent(s)
facilities: hot showers, toilets, electricity, free WiFi, washing machine, lots of shade, ~200m to beach
bestTime: Night 11 (Jun 26) — olive-grove camp near Petrovac, budget base for the Sveti Stefan/Budva day. Book ahead: +382 67 488 440 (campingmaslina.com).

name: Petrovac (relaxed, cheaper coast base)
description: Low-key resort town ~17km southeast of Budva with a small Venetian fortress (Kastio) on the seafront, a palm promenade, and two coves (its own beach + Lučice) within walking distance. A mellower, noticeably cheaper alternative base to Budva — central for Sveti Stefan, Skadar, Buljarica camping and the deep south.
communityNotes: Frequently recommended on forums as a calmer, more authentic and more affordable coast base than Budva, while still close to good beaches (Lučice, Buljarica, Drobni Pijesak). Reddit threads steering people away from Budva's tackiness often name Petrovac, Kotor and Perast as the nicer alternatives. The little Kastio fort is a free, photogenic sunset spot. Apartment reality: 4-person places here commonly ~€50-80/night in peak June vs ~€80-120 in central Budva/Kotor.
cost: 4-person apartments commonly ~€50-80/night in peak June (cheaper than Budva/Kotor)

name: Vitaljina Border Crossing (ME→HR, fast alternative)
description: Smaller ME→Croatia crossing 15km south of the busy Debeli Brijeg — typically 15-45 min wait vs 1-4h at Debeli Brijeg in June. Use this for the Jun 27 return to Dubrovnik area. D8 coastal road from Herceg Novi, crosses near Sutorina Bay.
communityNotes: 24/7, open year-round. Confirmed faster in June-July according to 2025-2026 reports. Cross before 8am or after 9pm either way. EES biometric check is now active (April 2026) at Croatian side — adds ~1-2 min per car, no special action needed. Live border cameras available at granicni-prelazi.eu.
bestTime: Day 12 (Jun 27) — use THIS crossing for ME→HR, not Debeli Brijeg (EES queues 1-4h). Leave Kotor by 15:00. 15–45 min wait. Drive D8 coastal road from Herceg Novi → Sutorina.
cost: Free (€1pp tourist tax payable cash)

name: Prutaš (2393m) — Best accessible Durmitor summit
description: Durmitor's best effort-to-reward ratio hike: 9km, 5-6h, no technical exposure. From Black Lake trailhead. Wild horses and goats on the open plateau, arguably the best panoramic view in the park with near-total solitude — far fewer people than Bobotov Kuk. Recommended over Veliki Međed for most groups.
communityNotes: Called 'arguably the best view in the entire park' with 'a feeling of complete isolation' (The Sandy Feet). The only challenge is LENGTH (long 4-hour grind), not terrain — no scrambling. One slightly unstable rubble section near the summit but nothing requiring hands. June: plateau melts early, no snow issues. Carry 2L water pp (no reliable sources on route). COMPARE WITH VELIKI MEĐED (same start, Black Lake): that route has UIAA-I scrambling and fixed cables — much harder. Prutaš is the everyone-can-do-it choice; do Veliki Međed only if the group is experienced with exposed scrambling.
cost: €5/pp NP entry + €2-3 parking
bestTime: Day 9 (Jun 24) — alternative Durmitor plateau walk if skipping Veliki Međed. Start by 07:00; no shelter on the open plateau if storms close in.

name: Piva Lake (turquoise reservoir swim — on route)
description: Intensely turquoise reservoir at 675m altitude, directly on the Šćepan Polje → Piva canyon → Žabljak road. Zero detour — it's the road. Plužine town has a beach on the lake. 18-22°C in June.
communityNotes: One of the most visually striking bodies of water on the entire trip — the turquoise color rivals Caribbean photos. Plužine is the main access point (beach, cafes). Free to swim. After the 56 dark tunnels of the Piva canyon, stopping here is genuinely rewarding. Allow 30-45 min.
cost: Free
bestTime: Day 8 (Jun 23) — natural stop after the Šćepan Polje border crossing and before the 56-tunnel Piva canyon drive to Žabljak. Midday sun = best turquoise color.

name: Tara River secret beach (below Đurđevića bridge)
description: Gravel river beach on the Tara canyon floor, accessed by a short gravel drive + 5-10min scramble from the P4 road south of Đurđevića Bridge. Glacial cold (7-12°C) — cold plunge of the trip. Best visited after rafting groups clear out in the afternoon.
communityNotes: Turn left before the P4 road tunnel south of Đurđevića Bridge, drive gravel track to end, then scramble down. The canyon walls tower above. Water is glacially cold — exhilarating not comfortable. Best after 15:00 when rafting groups have passed. Combine naturally with visiting the bridge.
cost: Free
bestTime: after 15:00

name: Bay of Kotor self-guided kayak (Go Go Gaga)
description: Paddle the Bay of Kotor to Perast and Our Lady of the Rocks under your own power — €25/day double kayak from Go Go Gaga near Kotor. Dawn start = mirror-flat bay, no crowds. Alternative to paying a boatman for the same route.
communityNotes: Dawn start (06:00-07:00) on a summer morning gives a completely different experience vs midday tourist boats. The bay is calm and mirror-flat before 08:00. Takes 1.5-2h to reach Perast by kayak. Operator: Go Go Gaga, booked via WhatsApp or their website. Good fitness required.
cost: ~€25/day double kayak
bestTime: dawn, before the boats start at 09:00

name: Rafting Camp Modra Rijeka (Blue River, Šćepan Polje)
description: Wooden cabin camp 300m from the Podgorica–Sarajevo road at Šćepan Polje, right on the Tara river — bookable independently. Won 'best rafting camp in region' at the International Tourism Fair 2022. Accommodation in cabin-style rooms (quad/triple/twin), plus restaurant, bar, WiFi, bike hire, full adventure menu. Alternative to Camp Grab next door.
communityNotes: Booking.com 9.1/20+ reviews: 'perfect value for money, delicious food, stunning mountain views.' TripAdvisor: 'rustic and quaint,' great food, staff praised. Easier to book independently than Grab's package deals. | Best used as an overnight before morning Tara rafting — operators are 300m away.
cost: ~€30 pp (~€120 for 4) — includes room; meals extra
facilities: cabin rooms, restaurant, bar, wifi, bike hire, guided activities

name: Auto Camp Radoman (Virpazar, Skadar Lake)
description: Family-run campsite ~3km from Virpazar on the edge of Skadar Lake National Park — the route skeleton's '~€15-20' option and the most-loved budget camp on the lake. Large trees, communal kitchen, tent units with kitchenettes also rentable. Full facilities: showers, electricity, wifi, laundry. Open year-round. Cash only. Owned by the Radoman family.
communityNotes: park4night 4.91/5 (11 reviews): 'Nice place with friendly hosts and some large trees for shade.' 'Lovely family run business — we were made very welcome with free glass of wine.' 'Two turtles living under the rocks.' 9.2/10 on Booking. | This is the campsite referenced in the route skeleton (Day 10 option).
bestTime: Walk-in or book Booking.com. Cash only on arrival — bring €20-25. Night 10 (Jun 25).
cost: ~€15–20 for 4 + tent + car (own tent); pre-pitched tent units also available ~€24
facilities: hot showers, toilets, electricity, wifi, laundry, communal kitchen, parking

name: Camp Virpazar (Boljevići, Skadar Lake)
description: Simple but 'lovingly maintained' family-run camp ~1.2km from Virpazar center at Boljevići — the most visible camp near the main kayak/boat tour launch point. ~20 pitches, shaded, quiet. German-speaking owners organize boat tours and kayaking. Season Apr 27–Oct 31.
communityNotes: Google 4.2/5 (113 reviews): 'Simple yet lovingly maintained family-run operation.' 'Clean facilities.' Homemade wine welcome. One reviewer noted 'no hot water' — may be inconsistent, verify on arrival. Access road: narrow single lane, sharp right after old stone bridge. Similar price to Camp Radoman.
cost: ~€20–25 for 4 + tent + car (June)
facilities: toilets, showers (verify hot water), electricity, wifi, bbq, washing machine

name: Auto Camp Rijeka Crnojevića (northern Skadar shore)
description: Lakeside campsite on the northern shore of Skadar Lake at the Rijeka Crnojevića river mouth — near the Pavlova strana serpentine viewpoint and the 15th-century historic fortress. Different zone from the Virpazar cluster (more secluded, further north). Restaurant on-site, kayaking, fishing, boat tours. Season Apr 15–Nov 30.
communityNotes: Detailed camplinq listing: electricity free, hot water free, wifi free, restaurant on-site. ~6000m² with tree shade. Price includes utilities. Note: less community reviews than Radoman/Virpazar — fewer independent confirms. Close to Pavlova strana viewpoint (already shortlisted).
cost: ~€25–35 for 4 + tent + car (includes electricity + hot water)
facilities: hot showers, toilets, electricity (free), wifi (free), restaurant, boat tours, kayak

name: Safari Beach Camping, Velika Plaža
description: Campsite on the beach in a pine forest on Velika Plaža, ~4km from Ada Bojana and 5km from Ulcinj old town. Sandy pitches under pines, direct beach access, restaurant and bar on-site. Open all year.
communityNotes: Balkan-turizam trip report (Serbian): '~€25/day for 4 including tax, vehicle, tent/caravan, electricity and sanitary access. New showers, hot water, very clean.' camping.info May 2025 review: 'Location excellent — directly on beach or in the pine forest. Sanitary facilities cleaned around the clock.' Dec 2025 reviewer: 'Good site with good pitches and facilities, open all year, bar on site.' One Aug 2025 reviewer noted WiFi didn't work and had a voltage issue on hook-up. Beach sections along Velika Plaža are literally named after the campsites here — you camp where you swim. Kitesurf rental centers nearby.
cost: ~€25–33 for 4 + tent + car (incl. electricity + tax; low season ~€25, peak July-Aug ~€33)
facilities: hot showers (new, cleaned around the clock), toilets, electricity hook-up, restaurant, beach bar, WiFi (unreliable), direct sandy beach access
bestTime: Day 11 (Jun 26) night — primary deep-south camp. Arrive before dusk, then drive/taxi ~10 min to Ada Bojana for Misko/Barbana dinner. Better facilities than the island FKK camp.

name: FKK Nudist Auto Camp, Ada Bojana island
description: Official campsite on Ada Bojana island (river-delta island 6km south of Ulcinj). 54,000 m² naturist camp with 3km sandy beach. Access via bridge/road; €5 island entry fee is deducted from your camping cost. Open 1 May – 15 Oct.
communityNotes: Official site (visitadabojana.com) price list: tent €5, car €4, per-person tax €1.20 — so 4 adults + tent + car = ~€25–26 in June (low-season rate; peak Jul 6–Aug 24 adds ~€2). Park4night (30 reviews, 3.1/5): 'new sanitary facilities with hot water now available' (May 2026 renovation confirmed); older reviews called facilities 'extremely old and partly desolate.' camping.info notes: mass communal showers (no individual cabins), sand everywhere, cleanliness inconsistent. Non-nudist: multiple reviewers confirm non-naturist beach sections exist and clothed visitors are accommodated — it's FKK-oriented but 'not mandatory.' Stilt-house fish restaurants (Misko, Ćirkova Čarda, Kod Ranka) on the island are excellent; the kitesurfing centres are actually at Velika Plaža (over the bridge), not the island itself. Mosquitoes in the evening. Water not drinkable on-site — bring your own.
cost: ~€24–26 for 4 + tent + car (tent €5, car €4, tourist tax €4.80 for 4) — June low-season rate; island entry €5 deducted from camp fee
facilities: communal showers (recently renovated 2025-2026, hot water now confirmed), toilets (4 bathrooms/16 toilets for ~220 guests), electricity hook-up (56 pitches), supermarket, restaurant, tennis courts — NO potable water on-site
bestTime: June pre-peak is quieter; naturist atmosphere; arrive before mosquito hour

name: Greenfield Campsite, Velika Plaža
description: New campsite (opened 2025) on road R-17 near Donji Štoj, 550m from Velika Plaža beach via shaded walking trail. Modern facilities, spacious illuminated pitches for tents and campervans, within walking distance of cafés and shops.
communityNotes: Greenfield's own site (2025 launch): 'clean showers and toilets, fresh water, wastewater disposal, electricity, BBQ, WiFi, children's play area.' Pricing 4 adults + own tent + car: tent €3 + car €4 + 4 adults × €5 = €27/night (plus ~€4 tourist tax optional). WhatsApp booking available (+382 68 725965). Not directly on the beach (550m walk) but far better maintained than MCM. This is the newest and most modern option in the area, with no bad reviews yet — but limited independent traveler data exists as of 2026.
cost: ~€27–31 for 4 + tent + car (tent €3, car €4, 4 × €5 per adult; tourist tax extra ~€4)
facilities: clean showers and toilets (new 2025), fresh water, electricity (€5/day), wastewater disposal, BBQ, WiFi, children's play area — 550m walk to beach
bestTime: June good — new site, likely less crowded than established camps

name: Rijeka Crnojevića — Danilo's Bridge swim + kayak village
description: Small historic village where the crystal-clear Rijeka Crnojevića river flows under a 19th-century stone arch bridge (Danilo's Bridge) into Skadar Lake. Rent kayaks to paddle out toward the lake, swimming en route; water is lake-warm (~24-26°C). The horseshoe bend viewpoint (15min walk uphill) is one of Montenegro's most iconic landscape photos.
communityNotes: Kayak hire from the village. Calm current, clear warm water — a striking contrast to the cold canyon rivers earlier in the trip. 'The view from the bend above the village is absolutely spectacular — totally worth the short climb.' NationalParksOfEurope confirms kayak rental here. Also: Danilo's Bridge itself is a photogenic arch over turquoise water. Add-on to the Skadar Lake kayak/winery day — not a standalone detour. Budget 1.5h including viewpoint.
cost: Kayak hire ~€10-15/h; viewpoint free
bestTime: morning or golden hour

name: Camping Dodosi (Skadar Lake NP)
description: Lakeside campsite inside Skadar Lake National Park at the confluence of the Karatuna River and the lake — swimming, kayaking, boat tours directly from camp. One of the cheapest overnight options on the whole trip with a genuinely spectacular NP setting.
communityNotes: park4night 4.4/5, 43 reviews: 'A paradise on the water in nature'; 'Beautifully located in the middle of nature'; hospitality described as 'really nice.' Small restaurant on-site serves fresh meals. ⚠️ ACCESS: narrow bridge ~2.3–2.4m wide on the approach road — a standard passenger car fits, but verify rental car width beforehand. Winding unpaved track after the bridge.
cost: ~€8/person + €4/camper — approx €36–40 total for 4 adults + tent. Electricity €3 extra.
facilities: Hot showers, toilets, waste disposal, WiFi/4G, small restaurant. Swimming direct from camp.
bestTime: Day 10 (Jun 25) — check in afternoon after Ostrog Monastery, kayak the lake at golden hour, swim. Pairs perfectly with Virpazar kayak rental (20 min drive). Bridge width: confirm car is under 2.2m wide.
